description: Canada implemented a ban on the manufacture and import of single-use plastics in 2022. The policy polarized small businesses, which argued against the feasibility of alternatives, versus environmentalists championing the reduction.

key_events.description: Government releases list of items to be banned, sparking industry pushback.
Legal challenges initiated by plastic manufacturers reach the courts.
Public confusion over alternative costs leads to a slight dip in support.

calibration_notes: Shows how abstract support for environment policies drops when implementation costs become tangible for small businesses.
